misconceptionSummary: Students combine unlike terms or multiply letters together when only addition or subtraction is involved.

steps.narration: This is a very common error. Three x plus two y does not simplify to five x y.

lines.text: They are different kinds of terms

steps.narration: These terms are not alike. One is an x term and one is a y term, so they cannot be added together into a single term.

lines.text: So the simplified expression is still 3x + 2y

steps.narration: If there are no like terms, the expression is already simplified. Here the correct answer stays as three x plus two y.
Compare the correct case. Three x plus two x can be collected because both terms are x terms. But three x plus two y cannot.

visuals.expression: Only combine terms with exactly the same variable part
visuals.label: No matching variable part means no collecting

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, always check whether the variable part matches exactly before collecting terms.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: treating addition of unlike terms as if it were multiplication. 3x + 2y is not 5xy. Unlike terms stay separate.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Do not invent a multiplication when the expression only shows addition. If the terms are unlike, leave them separate.
